name: Vince Guaraldi
abstract: Vincent Anthony Guaraldi (; né Dellaglio, July 17, 1928 – February 6, 1976) was an American jazz pianist who composed music for animated television adaptations of the Peanuts comic strip. His compositions for this series included their signature melody "Linus and Lucy" and the holiday standard "Christmas Time Is Here".

about_quotes.speaker: Keter Betts
about_quotes.evidence: We went to Europe with Woody Herman in ‘59... There was Jimmy Campbell on drums, Vince Guaraldi on piano... and Nat Adderley and Charlie Byrd and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keter Betts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: The players on here: ... Vince Guaraldi ... [INTERVIEWEE] I’m not sure. It seemed to me like it was Richie’s gig originally. He might have put me in as co-leader or something, because I wrote all the charts and I helped him – we talked over the instrumentation, who we were going to use.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Alley
about_quotes.evidence: He was a heck of a piano player. So he played with me. He played with my little outfit.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Alley interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: I took his place with Woody Herman... I got the gig. I got the gig. I played out there Sunday and all of a sudden I was working there with The Lighthouse All-Stars... primarily because of Vince remembering that I had mentioned that I was thinking of coming here.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: At the time he had Vince Guaraldi on piano... I didn't know who Cal Tjader was, but I went down there... I walked in and walked right up to Cal
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Shaffer
about_quotes.evidence: The composer was Vince Guaraldi, the great jazz pianist from San Francisco who wrote the score to the Peanuts stuff on TV.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Shaffer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 24, 2023
